title: cyrilla microareolata subsp. macrophylla
intro: Cyrilla microareolata subsp. macrophylla is a subspecies of Cyrilla microareolata, a plant species native to tropical regions. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Cyrillaceae family. Cyrilla microareolata subsp. macrophylla has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which have been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting immune function.
